title: [cross-file] lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64 pointer used after start() in standalone mode
description:
Interaction: main.c:init_standalone_lora <-> lora_l2.c:lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64

Bug: In main.c init_standalone_lora() (lines 87-131):
  Line 100: lichen_lora_l2_start()
  Line 106: eui64 = lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64()
  Lines 112-114: Use eui64 pointer for LOG_INF

After start(), the RX thread is running. The RX thread could theoretically trigger a state change to ABORTED if overflow is detected (lora_l2.c line 447). However, in ABORTED state, the EUI-64 is still valid (not cleared).

Actually, Round 1 already filed project-LICHEN-0li1.35 about this exact issue. Not filing duplicate.

Looking for other interactions...
close_reason: Duplicate of project-LICHEN-0li1.35 which already covers eui64 pointer usage after stop/deinit.
